ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Location context of where alcohol measure was taken
Highest breath alcohol concentration reading for the day
Highest raw transdermal alcohol sensor reading for the day
Breath alcohol concentration 60 minutes after alcohol administration
Breath alcohol concentration 75 minutes after alcohol administration
Breath alcohol concentration 105 minutes after alcohol administration
Breath alcohol concentration 135 minutes after alcohol administration
Breath alcohol concentration 180 minutes after alcohol administration
Visit name
Notes: e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2, Daily

ElementDescription: Breath alcohol concentration at arrival to study visit
Breath alcohol concentration prior to alcohol administration
Breath alcohol concentration 10 minutes after alcohol administration
Breath alcohol concentration 20 minutes after alcohol administration
Breath alcohol concentration 30 minutes after alcohol administration
Breath alcohol concentration 45 minutes after alcohol administration
